warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The fukui reactivity indices for furan were computed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) level of theory, including density fitting (RI/RIJK), in the gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1, using PySCF. The geometry was taken as-is from the provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz` file. The partial charges for the Fukui functions were computed using the Mulliken scheme.

The calculated Fukui indices and dual descriptors for each atom are:

| Atom | Fukui f+ (electrophilic) | Fukui f- (nucleophilic) | Fukui f0 (radical) | Dual Descriptor |
|:-----|:-------------------------|:------------------------|:-------------------|:----------------|
| O    | -1.3316                  | 0.0838                  | -0.6239            | -1.4154         |
| C    | 1.2287                   | 0.0891                  | 0.6589             | 1.1396          |
| C    | 1.2270                   | 0.0893                  | 0.6582             | 1.1377          |
| C    | 1.9233                   | 0.1615                  | 1.0424             | 1.7618          |
| C    | 1.9243                   | 0.1616                  | 1.0430             | 1.7627          |
| H    | -1.2191                  | 0.1025                  | -0.5583            | -1.3215         |
| H    | -1.2191                  | 0.1025                  | -0.5583            | -1.3216         |
| H    | -0.7668                  | 0.1048                  | -0.3310            | -0.8716         |
| H    | -0.7667                  | 0.1048                  | -0.3310            | -0.8715         |

The most electrophilic site is identified as the carbon atom at index 4 (1-based label 5) with a Fukui f+ value of 1.9242931.
The most nucleophilic site is identified as the carbon atom at index 4 (1-based label 5) with a Fukui f- value of 0.1616371.

The sums of the Fukui functions were checked for charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000 and sum(f-) = 1.0000, which indicates proper conservation.
